title: Inherent Vice
logline: A stoned private investigator in 1970 Gordita Beach is pulled into a labyrinthine conspiracy involving his ex-girlfriend, a missing real estate mogul, a mysterious heroin syndicate called the Golden Fang, and his nemesis—a straight-laced LAPD detective—as the counterculture era collapses into paranoia.
antagonistOrConflict: The Golden Fang syndicate and the systemic corruption it represents; also Detective Bigfoot Bjornsen as personal antagonist
tone: Hazy, paranoid, melancholic, darkly comic, elegiac for the death of the '60s counterculture
themes: The death of the counterculture and the '60s dream
Paranoia and conspiracy in Nixon-era America
The impossibility of truth in a corrupt system
The tension between hippie idealism and establishment control

actBreaks.description: Doc's investigation deepens after the Topanga Canyon encounter with Coy and the appearance of the sinister Golden Fang organization, shifting from personal mystery to systemic conspiracy.

validation.ruling: Scene #36 fundamentally shifts the investigation when Doc watches TV with Penny and recognizes the 'wild-eyed hippie' heckling Nixon as Coy Harlingen—the supposedly dead saxophonist. Penny reveals Coy is actually an informant infiltrating groups for police. This irreversible revelation transforms Doc's understanding from a simple missing persons case to a complex conspiracy involving law enforcement, forcing him into higher-stakes territory he cannot retreat from.

actBreaks.description: After Mickey Wolfmann resurfaces and Doc reunites with Shasta, the investigation's emotional and narrative center resolves, transitioning to the final confrontation with Adrian Prussia and the heroin.

validation.ruling: Scene #92 marks a major shift when Shasta mysteriously reappears at Doc's apartment after being missing, casually dismissing her relationship with Mickey as 'over' and claiming he's back with his family. This return fundamentally changes the story's direction from searching for Shasta to understanding what happened to her during her absence, setting up the final act's revelations about the Golden Fang's operations.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates like a contact high—it drifts, doubles back, and resists clean resolution. The structure is deliberately hazy and episodic, mirroring Doc's stoned POV and the paranoid logic of conspiracy. Act One establishes multiple mysteries (Shasta's visit, Mickey's disappearance, Glen's murder, Coy's fake death) that seem separate but gradually reveal themselves as tentacles of the same beast: the Golden Fang. The first act break occurs when Doc encounters Coy alive at Topanga Canyon and the Golden Fang shifts from rumor to tangible threat. Act Two is a sprawling investigation through institutions—the LAPD, the FBI, the rehab facility Chryskylodon—where Doc discovers everyone is compromised. The second act break comes after Shasta's return and Mickey's reemergence; the personal mystery resolves but the systemic one remains. Act Three accelerates into violence: Doc is drugged, nearly killed by Adrian Prussia, discovers Bigfoot's complicity, and brokers the heroin deal that saves Coy. The ending is ambiguous—Doc drives into fog with Shasta, their future uncertain. The script's non-linearity comes from its nested mysteries, unreliable narrator, drug-induced sequences, and the way past and present bleed together through Shasta.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately uneven, creating a narcotic rhythm. The first 30 pages move quickly through exposition and setup, then the script slows into a series of investigative scenes (scenes 19-35) that accumulate information without clear forward momentum—this is intentional, creating the feeling of being lost in the case. The Topanga Canyon sequence (scenes 38-44) provides a burst of energy and danger. The middle section (scenes 58-82) is the script's slowest stretch, as Doc tours institutions and the conspiracy becomes abstract. The pace accelerates dramatically in the final third: the PCP trip and Adrian Prussia confrontation (scenes 100-102) are visceral and violent, followed by rapid resolution scenes. The final driving scene (114) returns to the dreamy, elegiac tone of the opening.

incitingIncident.description: Shasta visits Doc at his apartment and tells him she needs help because her married lover Mickey Wolfmann's wife and her boyfriend may be plotting to commit him to an institution.

validation.ruling: Scene #2 shatters Doc's status quo when ex-girlfriend Shasta arrives after over a year asking for help with a scheme involving her married lover Mickey Wolfmann. This irreversibly launches the main investigation and establishes the central mystery that drives the entire plot.

midpoint.description: Doc observes a pristine dental operation room with rows of dentist chairs occupied by cleaned-up hippie-types being treated by doctors and masked assistants.

validation.ruling: Scene #58 merely shows Doc discovering a dental operation room, which is atmospheric but lacks transformative impact. Scene #59 provides the true midpoint revelation: Doc encounters Japonica Fenway who reveals she's escaping from Chryskylodon, a mysterious institution her parents keep sending her to. This discovery shifts Doc from reactive investigation to proactive pursuit of the Golden Fang conspiracy, raising personal stakes and introducing the institutional control theme central to the second half.

climax.description: Doc wakes handcuffed to a bed with his gun gone while Puck threatens to inject him with pure heroin before leaving to get it, and Doc desperately tries to break free from the handcuffs.

validation.ruling: Scene #101 is merely Doc arriving at an office and asking to see Adrian Prussia—setup, not confrontation. Scene #104 delivers the true climax: Doc wakes handcuffed to a bed with his gun gone while Puck threatens to inject him with pure heroin, creating the script's highest tension as Doc desperately tries to break free. This is the ultimate life-or-death confrontation where Doc faces the Golden Fang's lethal power directly.

scenes.heading: 1 OVER BLACK 1
scenes.summary: Sortilege narrates in voiceover that Shasta came to Doc's place after over a year, dressed differently than she used to.

scenes.heading: 2 INT. DOC’S APARTMENT (GORDITA BEACH) - DUSK (1970) 2
scenes.summary: Shasta visits Doc at his apartment and tells him she needs help because her married lover Mickey Wolfmann's wife and her boyfriend may be plotting to commit him to an institution.
scenes.turningPointNote: Inciting incident — Shasta brings Doc the case that launches the investigation

scenes.heading: 3 EXT. DOC’S - STREET - EVENING 3
scenes.summary: Doc walks Shasta to her car and she tells him he was always true to her before driving away, then he walks into town with Denis.

scenes.heading: 4 INT. PIPELINE PIZZA - NIGHT 4
scenes.summary: Doc eats pizza with Denis, Sortilege, and other locals at Pipeline Pizza and mentions seeing Shasta briefly.

scenes.heading: 5 INT. DOC’S APARTMENT 5
scenes.summary: Doc sits at home with his hair in knots, calls someone with no answer, and smokes a joint he's written his wish on that Shasta be safe.

scenes.heading: INT. AUNT REET’S HOUSE
scenes.summary: Doc visits Aunt Reet while she applies makeup and asks about Mickey Wolfmann; she tells him Wolfmann is a powerful real estate developer and warns Doc that Shasta should pay him.

scenes.heading: 6 EXT. LSD INVESTIGATIONS - DOC'S OFFICE - DAY 6
scenes.summary: Doc arrives at his office and Petunia tells him someone is waiting for him.

scenes.heading: 7 INT. DOC'S OFFICE - THAT MOMENT 7
scenes.summary: Tariq Khalil hires Doc to find Glen Charlock, an Aryan Brotherhood member who owes him money and works as a bodyguard for Mickey Wolfmann.
scenes.turningPointNote: Second case converges with first — Tariq connects Doc to Glen Charlock and Wolfmann

scenes.heading: INT. DOC'S CAR
scenes.summary: Doc drives to Channel View Estates while Sortilege narrates about the neighborhood being bulldozed for the development.

scenes.heading: 8 EXT. CHANNEL VIEW ESTATES - DAY 8
scenes.summary: Doc arrives at the under-construction Channel View Estates and parks near a makeshift mall including Chick Planet massage parlor with Harleys parked outside.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. CHICK PLANET MASSAGE PARLOR - DAY 9
scenes.summary: Doc enters Chick Planet massage parlor and asks Jade about Glen Charlock; she confirms Glen comes there and offers Doc a cigarette before the scene cuts.

scenes.heading: 10 ANGLE - LATER 10
scenes.summary: Doc regains consciousness with a lump on his head and steps outside.

scenes.heading: 11 EXT. CHANNEL VIEW ESTATES - DAY 11
scenes.summary: Doc emerges to find LAPD at a crime scene with Glen Charlock's body; Bigfoot confronts him through a bullhorn and has him arrested.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major escalation — Doc's client is found murdered and Doc becomes a suspect

scenes.heading: 12 ANGLE - LATER 12
scenes.summary: Doc sits in cuffs near Glen Charlock's bloody corpse on a gurney while Bigfoot mocks him and reveals the victim's identity.

scenes.heading: 13 INT. PARKER CENTER - DAY 13
scenes.summary: Bigfoot interrogates Doc at Parker Center about Glen Charlock's murder and Shasta's connection to Mickey Wolfmann, making sexual gestures and accusations.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. BIGFOOT'S EL CAMINO (MOVING) 14
scenes.summary: Bigfoot drives Doc in his El Camino and makes a sharp U-turn toward an undisclosed location.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. HILLS ABOVE VALLEY - DAY 15
scenes.summary: Bigfoot takes Doc to a secluded spot in the hills and tries to recruit him as a paid informant for federal drug enforcement funding.

scenes.heading: 16 INT. DOC'S PLACE - LATER 16
scenes.summary: Doc watches Bigfoot on TV news coverage of the crime scene, then receives calls from Tariq denying involvement and Bigfoot revealing Shasta has also disappeared.
scenes.turningPointNote: Stakes escalate — both Mickey Wolfmann and Shasta are now missing

scenes.heading: INT. DOC'S CAR A17
scenes.summary: Doc drives to Hope's house.

scenes.heading: 17 EXT. TORRANCE - SMALL HOUSE 17
scenes.summary: Doc visits Hope Harlingen who tells him about her husband Coy, a surf-sax musician she met while both were using heroin, and mentions Coy may have stayed in touch with Shasta.

scenes.heading: 18 AT THE DOOR - MOMENTS LATER 18
scenes.summary: As Doc leaves, he asks Hope how her husband knew Shasta and she says Shasta picked them up hitchhiking and may have stayed in touch with Coy.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. DOC'S OFFICE - DAY 19
scenes.summary: Doc arrives at his office and prepares to call Sloane Wolfmann.

scenes.heading: INT. DOC'S CAR A20
scenes.summary: Doc drives to Sloane Wolfmann's house in disguise while Sortilege narrates his plan to investigate whether Mickey is being held in a private institution.

scenes.heading: 20 INT. WOLFMANN MANSION (SANTA MONICA MOUNTAINS) - DAY 20
scenes.summary: Doc arrives at the Wolfmann mansion disguised as a psychiatrist and meets with Sloane Wolfmann by the pool where LAPD have set up; she denies any plot and dismisses him.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER 21
scenes.summary: Luz walks Doc to the front door and he asks to use the bathroom.

scenes.heading: 22 INT. PALATIAL BATHROOM - THAT MOMENT 22
scenes.summary: Doc snoops in a bathroom closet and discovers vintage silk ties hand-painted with nude women including Sloane and Luz; Luz appears and shows him hers.

scenes.heading: 23 EXT. WOLFMANN MANSION 23
scenes.summary: Doc encounters Bigfoot outside the Wolfmann mansion.

scenes.heading: 24 INT. LUNCH SPOT (DOWNTOWN) - DAY 24
scenes.summary: Doc meets Deputy DA Penny Kimball at a downtown lunch spot where she questions him about his relationship with Shasta and he admits Shasta contacted him about a plot against Mickey Wolfmann.

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. FEDERAL COURTHOUSE - DAY 25
scenes.summary: Penny leads Doc into the Federal Courthouse where FBI Agents Flatweed and Borderline intercept him to question him about Tariq Khalil.

scenes.heading: 26 INT. FEDERAL COURTHOUSE - OFFICE - DAY 26
scenes.summary: The FBI agents question Doc in an office about Tariq Khalil's visit and the Wolfmann case, but Doc refuses to share privileged client information.

scenes.heading: 27 EXT. DOC'S OFFICE - DAY 27
scenes.summary: Doc arrives at his office to find Jade and Bambi fleeing; Petunia hands him a flyer from Chick Planet with a message from Jade asking to meet at Club Asiatique.

scenes.heading: 28 INT. CLUB ASIATIQUE - NIGHT 28
scenes.summary: Doc meets Jade at Club Asiatique and she tells him to meet her outside.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. BEHIND THE CLUB/SAN PEDRO DOCKS - NIGHT 29
scenes.summary: Jade apologizes to Doc for helping the cops place him at the crime scene and warns him about the Golden Fang; she reveals Puck Beaverton knocked Doc out and mentions the Aryan Brotherhood connection to Mickey Wolfmann.
scenes.turningPointNote: Key revelation — Doc learns about the Golden Fang organization and gets crucial information about the conspiracy

scenes.heading: 30 EXT. BEACH/SAND DUNE - DAY 30
scenes.summary: Doc and Sauncho observe the schooner Golden Fang through binoculars from a beach sand dune while Sauncho explains the vessel's mysterious reputation.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. FISH PLACE 31
scenes.summary: At a fish restaurant, Sauncho tells Doc the Golden Fang's history as the ship Preserved and its connection to blacklisted actor Burke Stodger who fled the country.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. DOC'S PLACE - NIGHT 37
scenes.summary: Doc watches TV coverage of Nixon at a rally where a long-haired heckler screams obscenities; Penny emerges naked and identifies the heckler as Chucky.

scenes.heading: 38 EXT. TOPANGA CANYON HOUSE - AFTERNOON 38
scenes.summary: Doc and Denis, disguised as a reporter and photographer, arrive at a Topanga Canyon mansion where The Boards are staying and are greeted by groupies.

scenes.heading: 39 ANOTHER ANGLE 39
scenes.summary: Doc interviews Smedley from Spotted Dick who mentions paranoia at the house and grows quiet when road manager-types approach.

scenes.heading: 40 ANGLE - DOC 40
scenes.summary: Doc encounters Jade in a hallway who tells him she came with Bambi and directs him upstairs to find the person he met at Club Asiatique.

scenes.heading: 41 REHEARSAL STUDIO 41
scenes.summary: Doc finds Coy recording saxophone in a rehearsal studio and they speak in code about Hope being okay and street legal.

scenes.heading: 42 LIVING ROOM AREA 42
scenes.summary: Doc and Coy talk in a teepee where Coy reveals he can never return to his family because he's trapped in a gang-like organization and was recruited by Vigilant California to get clean.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major revelation — Coy is alive, trapped in Vigilant California, and connected to the larger conspiracy

scenes.heading: 43 IN ANOTHER PART OF THE HOUSE 43
scenes.summary: Denis photographs Coy in the dining room which triggers people to move toward them; Doc and Jade flee the house.

scenes.heading: 44 EXT. TOPANGA CANYON - NIGHT 44
scenes.summary: A sinister woodie with blacked-out windows follows Doc's car through Topanga Canyon while Denis and Jade flirt in the backseat.

scenes.heading: 45 JADE 45
scenes.summary: Jade gets out of the car and reveals her real name is Ashley, then tells Doc the Golden Fang is an Indochinese heroin cartel running a vertical operation.

scenes.heading: 46 EXT./INT. PARKER CENTER- DAY 46
scenes.summary: Doc walks into Parker Center while Sortilege narrates about the insular world of cop politics and loyalties.

scenes.heading: 47 INT. HOMICIDE ROOM 47
scenes.summary: Doc asks Bigfoot about resurrections and they discuss whether Coy's return from the dead is a con or mistaken identity.

scenes.heading: 48 ANOTHER ANGLE - LATER 48
scenes.summary: Doc shows Bigfoot a photo of Coy and asks him to look into it; Bigfoot agrees and invites Doc to follow him to the freezer.

scenes.heading: 49 INT. CORRIDOR/UTILITY ROOM - MOMENTS LATER 49
scenes.summary: Bigfoot shows Doc his freezer full of frozen chocolate bananas and sends them through pneumatic tubes, then his demeanor changes when he sees Adrian Prussia down the corridor.

scenes.heading: 50 EXT./INT. DOC’S OFFICE - NIGHT 50
scenes.summary: Clancy Charlock waits in Doc's locked office and hires him to find her brother Glen's killer, revealing Bigfoot referred her and that Puck Beaverton switched shifts with Glen the day he died.
scenes.turningPointNote: New client and revelation — Clancy provides crucial information about Puck Beaverton's role in Glen's murder

scenes.heading: 52 EXT. DOC'S OFFICE - NIGHT 52
scenes.summary: Clancy and Doc step outside where her two bikers wait; she yells over the loud engines that Puck switched shifts with Glen and probably lives in Ojai.

scenes.heading: INT. DOC'S CAR - NIGHT A53
scenes.summary: Doc drives alone after leaving Clancy, heading to retrieve a postcard.

scenes.heading: 53 EXT. DOC'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 53
scenes.summary: Doc approaches his apartment noticing suspicious shadows, finds a postcard from Shasta expressing regret and nostalgia for their past together.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. DOC'S APARTMENT 54
scenes.summary: Doc enters his apartment and lights a joint while Shasta's voiceover recalls a memory about using a Ouija board during a time when no one had any dope.

scenes.heading: 56 EXT. SUNSET (NEAR VERMONT) 56
scenes.summary: Doc and Shasta arrive in the rain at an address that turns out to be a huge empty excavated lot filled with water.

scenes.heading: 57 EXT. SUNSET (NEAR VERMONT) (PRESENT) 57
scenes.summary: Doc walks to where the vacant lot used to be and finds a futuristic six-story building shaped like a golden fang; he tells Denis to wait in the car while he investigates.
scenes.turningPointNote: Doc discovers the Golden Fang headquarters, a major location in the conspiracy he's investigating.

scenes.heading: 58 INT. GOLDEN FANG ENTERPRISES H.Q. - THAT MOMENT 58
scenes.summary: Doc enters the quiet Golden Fang Enterprises headquarters and tells receptionist Xandra he's there to pick up a package; she calls someone and leads him down a hall.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. OFFICE - THAT MOMENT 59
scenes.summary: Xandra places Doc in an empty office where Dr. Blatnoyd enters and asks for ID; Doc presents a Chinese business card which Blatnoyd cannot read, leading to confusion about Doc's ethnicity.

scenes.heading: 60 INT. HUGE ROOM - THAT MOMENT 60
scenes.summary: Doc observes a pristine dental operation room with rows of dentist chairs occupied by cleaned-up hippie-types being treated by doctors and masked assistants.
scenes.turningPointNote: Doc discovers the Golden Fang is running a large-scale dental operation, revealing a key piece of the organization's activities.

scenes.heading: 61 BACK IN RUDY'S OFFICE 61
scenes.summary: Doc returns to Blatnoyd's office where he encounters Japonica Fenway, a young woman he previously saved; she tells him she's escaping from Chryskylodon, a place her parents keep sending her.

scenes.heading: 62 IN THE LOBBY - AT THAT MOMENT 62
scenes.summary: Denis wanders the lobby holding Doc's steering wheel and calling for him while Xandra chases after him zipping up her skirt.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. DR. BLATNOYD’S OFFICE 63
scenes.summary: Denis enters Blatnoyd's office to tell Doc the car is damaged; Blatnoyd reveals Japonica has been institutionalized and Denis asks if she received electroshock therapy.

scenes.heading: 64 INT. 1960 MERCEDES - EVENING 64
scenes.summary: The group gets into a Mercedes and Dr. Blatnoyd pushes a market bag under the seat, refusing to explain its contents when Denis asks.

scenes.heading: 65 INT. 1960 MERCEDES (DRIVING) 65
scenes.summary: Japonica drives without headlights while Doc and Blatnoyd try to get her to turn them on; she runs a red light and claims it was the eye of something watching her.

scenes.heading: 66 EXT./INT. STREET - MERCEDES - NIGHT 66
scenes.summary: Police pull them over with guns raised and shaking; Japonica asks if they're the Great Beast while Blatnoyd lies about her sister being in labor.

scenes.heading: 67 EXT. BEL AIR MANSION - NIGHT 67
scenes.summary: They arrive at a Bel Air mansion where Blatnoyd retrieves his bag and enters through the gate; Japonica emotionally questions whether her father wanted her back and Doc gives her his card.

scenes.heading: 68 EXT. DOC'S OFFICE - DAY/NIGHT (NEXT NIGHT?) 68
scenes.summary: Doc walks past Petunia who opens her legs without underwear trying to delay him from entering his office.

scenes.heading: 69 CLANCY CHARLOCK AND TARIQ KHALIL 69
scenes.summary: Doc opens his office door to find Clancy Charlock and Tariq Khalil having sex on his desk.

scenes.heading: 70 INT. DOC'S OFFICE - LATER 70
scenes.summary: Doc makes coffee while Tariq confesses that Glenn didn't owe him money but guns and bazookas for WAMBAM, a militant Black organization.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tariq reveals the truth about what Glenn owed him, connecting the case to weapons trafficking and militant groups.

scenes.heading: 71 OMITTED 71
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: 72 INT. PLASTIC NICKEL DINER - NIGHT 72
scenes.summary: Doc sits at a diner with Clancy and Tariq in the bathroom while Thomas Jefferson appears and discusses guns, opium, and revolution with Doc.

scenes.heading: 73 INT. DOC'S APARTMENT - LATER THAT NIGHT 73
scenes.summary: Doc sits sleeping upright when the phone rings and he answers.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. BIGFOOT'S HOUSE - SAME TIME 74
scenes.summary: Bigfoot calls Doc to inform him that Dr. Blatnoyd is dead from a fatal neck injury near a trampoline and that Doc is a suspect because he was seen with Blatnoyd.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blatnoyd's death raises the stakes significantly and puts Doc directly in danger as a murder suspect.

scenes.heading: INT. DOC'S CAR A75
scenes.summary: Doc drives to meet Bigfoot at a Japanese diner with Sortilege watching him quietly.

scenes.heading: 75 INT. JAPANESE COFFEE SHOP - DAY 75
scenes.summary: Bigfoot eats pancakes with two forks and delivers a monologue about fear spreading through the town; he reveals Coy Harlingen's case is officially cleared as an overdose but shares crime scene details suggesting otherwise.

scenes.heading: 76 OMITTED 76
scenes.summary: Scene omitted from script.

scenes.heading: INT. DOC'S CAR (DRIVING) R76
scenes.summary: Doc and Sortilege drive to Chryskylodon in a canyon area; Sortilege reveals the name means 'animal tooth made out of gold' in ancient Greek, not 'serenity.'

scenes.heading: EXT. GATES AT CHRYSKYLODON - DAY A77
scenes.summary: Doc goes through a security check at Chryskylodon's gates, patted down by armed guards.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHRYSKYLODON - DAY B77
scenes.summary: Doc drives up to the Chryskylodon building in an establishing shot.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHRYSKYLDON - DAY C77
scenes.summary: Doc arrives and is greeted with handshakes outside the facility.

scenes.heading: 77 INT. DINING ROOM - DAY 77
scenes.summary: Dr. Threeply gives Doc a tour through the dining room, showing off the administrative lounge and vineyard wine.

scenes.heading: 78 INT. OTHER AREA - DAY 78
scenes.summary: The tour continues past a photo of Sloane Wolfmann and a sign for a new wing funded by a devoted friend; Doc sees kids cleaning and feels a creepy sensation about the place.

scenes.heading: INT. MOVIE THEATER - LOBBY/HALLWAY - DAY A79
scenes.summary: Doc walks down a hallway with a staff assistant who questions why he came all the way to Chryskylodon instead of using Bay Area facilities.

scenes.heading: 79 INT. STEAM TUNNEL - DAY 79
scenes.summary: Dr. Threeply shows Doc the Advanced Therapy Group in a steam tunnel where Doc spots Coy Harlingen among chanters in white robes and sees an orderly wearing a tie painted with naked Shasta.
scenes.turningPointNote: Doc discovers Coy Harlingen alive at Chryskylodon and sees evidence connecting Shasta to the facility through the painted tie.

scenes.heading: 80 INT. LOBBY 80
scenes.summary: A staff associate interrupts the tour to report a problem with the electroshock machine; Dr. Threeply excuses himself and Doc begins snooping.

scenes.heading: 81 INT. HALLWAY - DAY 81
scenes.summary: Doc walks down a hallway where Coy pulls him into a room and explains he's there for a bi-monthly checkup as part of being dead; Coy reveals he works as a spy with a gift for alternate personalities and claims it's a higher discipline than his former addictions.

scenes.heading: EXT. GROUNDS - DAY A82
scenes.summary: Doc follows Puck across the grounds.

scenes.heading: 82 EXT. ANOTHER AREA - DAY 82
scenes.summary: Doc follows Puck through water to a bungalow surrounded by FBI agents where he finds Mickey Wolfmann lounging on a patio; Mickey tells Doc they're helping him wake from his bad hippie dream and that he dreamed of giving away all his money.
scenes.turningPointNote: Doc discovers Mickey Wolfmann alive and being held/reprogrammed by the FBI, solving the central missing person case.

scenes.heading: 85 INT. FBI - HOLDING ROOM - DAY (VERSION 1) 85
scenes.summary: FBI agents Flatweed and Borderline question Doc about his inappropriate curiosity regarding Michael Wolfmann; Doc denies being paid to investigate the case.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLDING AREA (VERSION 2)
scenes.summary: FBI agent Flatweed questions Doc about recognizing Mickey Wolfmann and warns him his curiosity about the case is inappropriate.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRYSKOLODON - HOLDING AREA - LATER (VERSION 3)
scenes.summary: Flatweed and Borderline question Doc in a holding area about his pursuit of Mickey Wolfmann despite not being paid for the case.

scenes.heading: 88 CLOSEUP - NEWSPAPERS 88
scenes.summary: Newspapers reveal Mickey Wolfmann has re-emerged to open a new casino in Las Vegas; voiceover explains he's been reprogrammed by the FBI and returned to his greedy ways with Sloane.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mickey's public reappearance and transformation marks the resolution of his disappearance and reveals the FBI's successful control over him.

scenes.heading: 89 INT. DOC'S APARTMENT - DUSK 89
scenes.summary: Doc watches TV at home when Shasta appears in beach wear; she tells him she's been up north with family and that her relationship with Mickey is over.

scenes.heading: 90 ANOTHER ANGLE 90
scenes.summary: The phone rings and Doc answers while Shasta moves around the apartment getting a beer and smoking a joint.

scenes.heading: INT. BIGFOOT'S HOUSE
scenes.summary: Bigfoot calls asking about Shasta and developments on the Coy Harlingen case; when Doc mentions the Fang marks and suggests Bigfoot might face a hearing, Bigfoot goes silent.

scenes.heading: 92 SAME SCENE - LATER 92
scenes.summary: Doc and Shasta are together in bed; she reveals she went on a boat ride where she was told she was precious cargo that couldn't be insured because of inherent vice, and confesses she introduced Coy to Burke Stodger who set him up with the Vigilant California group.
scenes.turningPointNote: Shasta reveals her direct connection to Coy's fake death and work with Vigilant California, deepening her involvement in the conspiracy.

scenes.heading: 93 BEACH 93
scenes.summary: Doc and Shasta walk on the beach while voiceover reflects on Doc's realization that he's been working for rich clients rather than the poor people he thought he was helping.

scenes.heading: 94 EXT. WASTE A PERP SHOOTING RANGE - DAY (OR INT. PHONE 94
scenes.summary: Doc visits Bigfoot at a shooting range and confronts him about using Doc to get to Adrian Prussia because Bigfoot can't approach Prussia directly without getting in trouble.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. HALL OF JUSTICE - DAY 95
scenes.summary: Doc visits deputy DA Penny Kimball at the Hall of Justice asking to see someone's locked file; she agrees to help and suggests they have dinner at her place with pizza.

scenes.heading: 96 CLOSEUP - DOC 96
scenes.summary: Doc examines Adrian Prussia's internal affairs file and sees a photo of him aboard the Golden Fang; voiceover reveals Prussia worked as a loan shark and contract killer for the LAPD whose cases never made it to trial.
scenes.turningPointNote: Doc discovers Adrian Prussia's role as an LAPD contract killer and his connection to the Golden Fang, revealing deep police corruption.

scenes.heading: 97 EXT. AP FINANCE - DAY 97
scenes.summary: Doc arrives at Adrian Prussia Finance and parks while noticing a bunch of men loitering around who notice him back; voiceover reflects that Doc has been avoiding this confrontation.

scenes.heading: 98 INT. AP FINANCE - DAY 98
scenes.summary: Doc arrives at AP Finance and tells the secretary he's there to see Adrian Prussia regarding Bigfoot Bjornsen.

scenes.heading: 99 INT. ADRIAN'S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER 99
scenes.summary: Doc meets with Adrian Prussia in an office covered with baseball bats, where Adrian recognizes him from Fritz's shop and questions who Doc is working for, but Doc's response is cut off.

scenes.heading: 100 INT. ROOM WITH WINDOWS 100 100
scenes.summary: Doc experiences a PCP trip where he sees two versions of himself and encounters a large black-hooded figure with no face identified as the Golden Fang.

scenes.heading: 101 ANOTHER ANGLE - LATER 101 101
scenes.summary: Doc wakes handcuffed to a bed with his gun gone while Puck threatens to inject him with pure heroin before leaving to get it, and Doc desperately tries to break free from the handcuffs.
scenes.turningPointNote: Doc is captured and faces imminent death by forced heroin overdose — a critical moment of mortal danger

scenes.heading: 102 INSIDE THE GARAGE 102 102
scenes.summary: Doc discovers Bigfoot unloading heroin from Adrian's car into his own Impala and shooting two people, then Bigfoot tells Doc to take care of them.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bigfoot is revealed to be deeply corrupt, killing people and stealing heroin — a major revelation that shifts Doc's understanding of the conspiracy

scenes.heading: 103 INT. BIGFOOT'S CAR (MOVING) 103 103
scenes.summary: Bigfoot drives Doc to retrieve his impounded car while Doc questions where they're going.

scenes.heading: 104 EXT. CANOGA PARK TOW YARD - NIGHT 104 104
scenes.summary: At the tow yard, Bigfoot secretly plants a kilo of heroin in Doc's trunk before giving him his car keys and driving off.

scenes.heading: 105 INT. DENIS' APARTMENT - NIGHT 105 105
scenes.summary: Doc hides the heroin in a cardboard TV box at Denis' apartment with Denis and Jade present.

scenes.heading: 106 INT. DOC'S PLACE - MORNING 106 106
scenes.summary: Doc receives a phone call from Crocker Fenway, who reveals he represents people who want back something Doc has and proposes a meeting.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. RESTAURANT (LOCATION TBD) - LATER 107 107
scenes.summary: Doc meets Crocker Fenway at a restaurant where Fenway discusses Rudy Blatnoyd's death, threatens Doc's life, and demands the heroin in exchange for not harming Shasta.

scenes.heading: 108 EXT. SHOPPING MALL (ARTESIA AND HAWTHORNE) - MORNING 108 108
scenes.summary: Doc, Denis, and Jade wait in a parking lot and hand over the heroin to a blond family in a Buick station wagon who are revealed to be Golden Fang operatives.

scenes.heading: 109 EXT. TOPANGA CANYON HOUSE - DAY 109 109
scenes.summary: Doc picks up Coy from the Topanga Canyon house where a Buddhist priest is performing an exorcism, and Coy tells him he's officially off everyone's payroll.

scenes.heading: 110 EXT. COY/HOPE'S HOUSE - LATER AFTERNOON 110 110
scenes.summary: Doc drops Coy off at his house where Hope and Amethyst greet him, and Hope and Coy embrace while Hope waves to Doc.

scenes.heading: 111 FISH PLACE - DOC AND SAUNCHO - DAY 111 111
scenes.summary: Doc asks Sauncho about an inherent vice clause, then Sauncho receives a phone call and they both run out.

scenes.heading: 112 EXT. SAN PEDRO - DAY 112 112
scenes.summary: Doc and Sauncho watch from shore as Department of Justice and Coast Guard boats seize the Golden Fang ship, and Sauncho explains the legal implications of abandoned vessels.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Golden Fang ship is seized by authorities — a major blow to the syndicate and resolution of a central mystery

scenes.heading: 113 INT. DOC'S PLACE - DAY 113 113
scenes.summary: Bigfoot smashes down Doc's door and enters, they both apologize simultaneously for the previous night, then Bigfoot eats a large amount of Doc's marijuana and they share a moment of connection.

scenes.heading: 114 INT. DOC'S CAR (DRIVING) - NIGHT 114 114
scenes.summary: Doc drives Shasta on the foggy freeway as she reminisces about their past and falls asleep, while Doc joins a slow convoy through the fog.
